title: euphorbia × budensis
intro: Euphorbia × budensis, also known as the Spurge or Spotted Spurge, is a hybrid plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and spotted or marbled leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Euphorbia × budensis include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken orally, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
